businessOverview: Apple Inc. is the world's largest consumer electronics and devices company by revenue, designing, manufacturing and selling smartphones (iPhone), personal computers (Mac), tablets (iPad), wearables and home/accessory products, alongside a fast-growing high-margin Services business. The company runs a tightly integrated hardware-software-services model anchored by the iPhone, which remains roughly half of total revenue. In FQ2 2026 (quarter ended March 28, 2026) Apple posted record March-quarter revenue of $111.2B (+17% YoY) with all five product categories and all five geographic segments growing, fueled by 'off the charts' demand for the iPhone 17 lineup and an all-time Services record. The company is in the midst of a historic leadership transition: on April 20, 2026 Apple announced Tim Cook will become Executive Chair and hardware chief John Ternus will become CEO effective September 1, 2026.
productModel: Apple's model combines premium hardware (iPhone ~51% of revenue, plus Mac, iPad, Wearables/Home/Accessories) with an increasingly dominant Services layer (App Store, AppleCare, advertising, cloud, payments, and subscriptions such as iCloud+, Apple Music, Apple TV+, Apple Arcade, Apple Fitness+, and the Apple Card/Apple Pay financial ecosystem). Services carry far higher gross margins than hardware and reached an all-time record $31B in FQ2 2026 (~28% of revenue), driving total company gross margin to 49.3%. The installed base of active devices continues to set records, providing a durable recurring-revenue flywheel. Hardware is largely manufactured by contract partners (Foxconn, Pegatron, etc.), with assembly increasingly shifting from China toward India and Vietnam; iPhone application processors are fabricated exclusively by TSMC.
marketContext: Apple competes across consumer electronics, mobile OS, and digital services. It faces intensifying pressure on three fronts in 2026: (1) AI — Apple is widely viewed as a laggard in generative AI and has turned to a multiyear ~$1B/year Google Gemini partnership to power a delayed, revamped Siri expected to ship in iOS 26.5 (mid-2026) with a fuller conversational version in iOS 27; (2) Regulation — the EU Digital Markets Act forced repeated App Store fee restructurings (a 5% Core Technology Commission replacing the per-install fee in Jan 2026) plus a EUR500M fine, while US court rulings have loosened App Store anti-steering rules; (3) Supply chain & geopolitics — a global memory shortage driven by AI demand and Trump-era tariffs on India (up to 50%) and threats of 25% tariffs on non-US-made iPhones pressure margins and manufacturing strategy. China returned to strong growth (+28% YoY to $20.5B) on iPhone 17 demand and local subsidies.
competitors: Alphabet/Google (Android, Pixel, Gemini AI, search default)
Xiaomi / Vivo / Oppo (China and emerging-market smartphones)
Spotify / Netflix (Services: music and video streaming)
customers: Global consumers (premium smartphone, PC, tablet, and wearables buyers)
Enterprise and education institutions (Mac and iPad deployments)
Services subscribers across iCloud+, Apple Music, Apple TV+, AppleCare, advertising
verticals: Digital Payments / Fintech (Apple Pay, Apple Card)

leadership.name: Tim Cook
leadership.title: CEO (transitioning to Executive Chair effective Sept 1, 2026)
leadership.background: CEO since 2011; on April 20, 2026 Apple announced Cook, 65, will become Executive Chair of the Board on Sept 1, 2026, remaining involved in strategy, policy and oversight while John Ternus becomes CEO. Cook continues as CEO through summer 2026 for a smooth handover.

leadership.name: John Ternus
leadership.title: Incoming CEO (effective Sept 1, 2026); currently SVP Hardware Engineering
leadership.background: Ternus, 50-51, joined Apple in 2001 and has led Hardware Engineering since 2021, overseeing iPhone, iPad, Mac and Apple silicon hardware. Named CEO and board member effective Sept 1, 2026 following a long-term succession process approved unanimously by the board.

leadership.name: Kevan Parekh
leadership.title: Senior Vice President & Chief Financial Officer
leadership.background: Became CFO on Jan 1, 2025, succeeding Luca Maestri (CFO 2014-2024). Joined Apple in 2013 and led financial planning, business planning and product development planning. Continues as CFO under the new leadership structure.

leadership.name: Arthur (Art) Levinson
leadership.title: Chair of the Board (becomes Lead Independent Director on Sept 1, 2026)
leadership.background: Chairman of Apple's board; transitions to Lead Independent Director when Tim Cook assumes the Executive Chair role on the Sept 1, 2026 transition date.

segments.name: iPhone
segments.note: +21.7% YoY; March-quarter record on 'off the charts' iPhone 17 demand; ~51% of total revenue; sales partly held back by supply constraints on advanced TSMC processor nodes.

segments.name: Services
segments.note: +16.3% YoY; all-time record with all-time highs in most categories, broad-based across developed and emerging markets; key gross-margin driver.

segments.name: Mac
segments.note: +5.7% YoY; growth on continued M-series / Apple silicon demand.
